rationale: A visible buildup along the Tethys border will deter further provocations and signal unwavering resolve to defend national interests.
Deploying a flotilla to the Eastern Tethys Sea denies adversary maneuver space and protects critical energy transit routes.
Disabling Tethys’ command-and-control systems in border regions creates asymmetric leverage without triggering full-scale escalation.
Covertly disrupting Tethyian supply lines and communication hubs degrades operational readiness while maintaining plausible deniability.
Purge suspected foreign assets within defense and energy ministries to prevent leakage of strategic intentions.
Stage a minor, deniable incident to justify future military actions and sway domestic opinion toward escalation.
Secure reserves of critical energy inputs now to insulate the economy from potential Tethys-induced supply shocks.
Lock in alternative export routes and energy transit agreements to reduce dependency on Tethys pipelines.
Flood Tethys’ financial system with destabilizing capital flows to erode confidence in their currency and deter aggressive posturing.
Deter Novaris’ regrouping by visibly reinforcing our defensive posture along the Tethys border to signal resolve.
Project power asymmetrically by deploying fast-attack craft to secure maritime supply routes and threaten Novaris’ energy logistics.
Disrupt Novaris’ military logistics planning systems to delay redeployment and exacerbate their current strain.
Exploit Novaris’ logistical vulnerabilities by covertly disrupting fuel and ammunition depots near the Tethys front.
Strengthen allied trust and multilateral response by revealing Novaris’ recalibration plans through secure channels.
Orchestrate a deniable incident to expose Novaris’ aggression narrative as hollow, swaying neutral opinion.
Bypass premature peace overtures by quietly building a coalition to coordinate sanctions and diplomatic isolation of Novaris.
Position ourselves as responsible actors by offering neutral mediation, enhancing international legitimacy regardless of Novaris’ response.
Soften institutional resistance in key democracies by accelerating people-to-people ties with influential partner states.
Counteract dwindling reserves by accelerating stockpiles of critical energy and military inputs before Novaris can disrupt supply.
Secure alternative export routes and emergency supply deals with Meridian to reduce dependency on Novaris-linked infrastructure.
Stabilize domestic confidence and fund defense spending through patriotic, high-yield bonds targeting domestic savers and diaspora communities.
